## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-08 (batch 227 ┬Ę 515 + 634 merged)

Both merged. 634 (#1167, `877f0173`) ŌåÆ 515 (#1168, `25f7a8b9`); claim-stake #1166 (`9a0b2990`).

- **515** (NIST CSF 2.0 Tier/Profile assessment) ŌĆö **MERGED**. CSF maturity construct (P0-480-6): Tier (1-4) + Current/Target Profiles + gap view. JUDGMENT D1: CSF-specific tables, not a generalized maturity engine (Article VII). Invariant #6: 4 tenant-scoped tables `FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`; `TestRLS_CrossTenantIsolation` + `TestRLS_DenyOnMissingContext` prove isolation. Invariant #1: gap view reuses the crosswalk traversal. 7 `/v1/csf/**` routes (openapi ŌåÆ 236); migration `20260608080000_csf_tier_profile.sql`; new `internal/api/csfassessment` enrolled in shards leg A; web assessment page. **Constitutional guard refined (D7):** CI surfaced `TestImport_NoDirectRequirementToRequirementTableExists` (invariant-#7 guard) tripping on `csf_profile_selections`' FK to `framework_requirements` ŌĆö that FK is assessment-stateŌåÆreference-data, NOT a reqŌåÆreq crosswalk. Guard refined to count only CATALOG-originating FKs (discriminator = tenancy: crosswalks are tenant-agnostic, assessment-state is tenant-scoped); a rolled-back positive sub-assertion proves it still catches a real crosswalk. Engineer made the call + recorded it.
- **634** (Azure Firewall rule-collection-group cursor pagination) ŌĆö **MERGED**. ARM `nextLink` paging on both the firewallPolicies list + per-policy rule-collection-group reads (mirrors Intune `@odata.nextLink` idiom). Record caps kept as DoS backstop; new page caps terminate a self-pointing nextLink. Over-collection guard unchanged (no field/schema change); GET-only.

## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-08 (batch 226 ┬Ę 614 + 631 merged)

Both merged. 631 (#1163, `7fee2bd2`) ŌåÆ 614 (#1164, `1706ccbe`); claim-stake #1162 (`78c8155f`).

- **631** (CI fail-closed merge-gate) ŌĆö **MERGED**. Mechanism (a) aggregator + narrow (c) canary. Additive only (no existing required job renamed). `CI ┬Ę merge-gate` short-circuits green on docs-only PRs, requires every one of 9 required legs to be exactly `success` on a Go-affecting PR (failure/cancelled/skipped-while-needed ŌåÆ fail closed ŌĆö the exact slice-474 hole). Matrix shard `result` is the aggregate so one RED shard ŌćÆ gate RED. `tests-integration-main-canary` re-runs the shard matrix on main pushes unconditionally with `cancel-in-progress:false`. **HAND-OFF:** maintainer must add `CI ┬Ę merge-gate` to branch-protection required-contexts (advisory until then).
- **614** (Azure Firewall rule-collection evidence) ŌĆö **MERGED**. New sibling kind `azure.firewall_rules.v1` on `atlas-azure`: lists `Microsoft.Network/firewallPolicies` + bounded per-policy rule-collection-group read; anchors `[NET-04, NET-01]`. Structural over-collection guard (no field for flow logs/captures/traffic/NAT secrets/threat-intel/route tables; reflection test pins it); ARM Reader, GET-only. No migration (schemaregistry DefaultSeed). Spillover 634.

## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-08 (batch 225 ┬Ę 615 + 620 + 633 merged)

All merged. 633 (#1160, `90b1416a`) ŌåÆ 615 (#1154, `a6a173de`) ŌåÆ 620 (#1155, `3d690943`); claim-stake #1153 (`567febe5`); 633 spec #1156.

- **633** (fix 474 ingest/verify hash round-trip) ŌĆö **MERGED**. Root cause confirmed by byte-diff: `observed_at` sole divergent field (proto-nanos hashed at ingest, ┬Ąs-truncated by `TIMESTAMPTZ`, lossily reconstructed at verify). Fix: additive nullable `observed_at_nanos BIGINT` (`UnixNano()`), reconstructed faithfully with legacy-NULL fallback to the lossy column. `HashRecord` untouched ŌåÆ cross-language client receipt-hash contract byte-identical. New host-clock-independent pure-Go round-trip guard (the regression net 474 lacked) + integration tamper assertion. Migration `20260608070000_evidence_observed_at_nanos.sql`. **Why 474 escaped:** macOS `time.Now()` is ┬Ąs-aligned so 474's local run passed; CI-Linux (full nanos) went RED and 474 merged red anyway. Proven: shard A green on 633's PR.
- **615** (Azure KV RBAC role-assignment enumeration) ŌĆö **MERGED**. RBAC-mode vault ŌåÆ second read-only ARM read of `Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments` scoped to each vault, merged into `access_entries` as `principal_type=rbac_role_assignment`. No schema change; structural metadata-only over-collection guard (no field for secret material); ARM Reader only. Spillover 623.
- **620** (map unmapped vendor claim ŌåÆ SCF anchor) ŌĆö **MERGED**. `PATCH /v1/oscal/component-claims/{id}/scf-anchor` (approver-gated, RLS); invariant #7 (requirementŌåÆanchor only); no fabricated coverage (integration test asserts `control_evaluations` stays empty); audit via generalized slice-589 event log; web anchor-picker. Migration `20260608060000_*`; openapi regenerated.

## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-08 (batch 222 ┬Ę 520 + 613 + 474 merged)

All three merged. 520 (#1143, `e91f9ed6`) + 613 (#1141, `b0f41c87`) + 474 (#1142, `ea3541fb`); claim-stake #1140 (`2f988d93`).

- **520** (Azure NSG/firewall rule evidence) ŌĆö **MERGED**. `azure.nsg_rules.v1` (4th Azure kind): per-NSG security-rule posture (direction/access/protocol/ports/priority + association counts). Structural over-collection guard ŌĆö structs carry rule metadata only (no flow-logs/packet/traffic-content/secrets, reflection-test-enforced); ARM client GET-only. Anchors `[NET-04, NET-01]`. Descriptive verdict flags 22/3389-from-Internet. Spillover 614.
- **613** (web Settings control for `bundle_gate_mode`) ŌĆö **MERGED**. A `<select>` (strict/advisory/mandatory_tests + per-mode explanation) in the admin-gated tenant settings, driving slice-608's existing `PATCH /v1/tenants/{id}` (web-only, no Go changes; the slice-144 BFF route already forwards the field). Hermetic Playwright spec route-mocks the GET (b219 lesson). No spillover.
- **474** (ingest evidence-hash alignment) ŌĆö **MERGED**. Real correctness fix: ingest hashed the record WITH its wire `scope` but the ledger discarded the scope, so the slice-464 ledger-wide verify walk never validated production records. Fixed additively ŌĆö new nullable `scope_canonical JSONB` (migration `20260608040000`) + a shared `internal/canonjson` codec written at ingest + rehydrated at verify; the published EVIDENCE_SDK receipt-hash wire contract is unchanged. **Invariant #2 (append-only) respected** ŌĆö zero evidence-row mutation; legacy NULL-scope rows fall back to the scope-free reconstruction. Tamper-detection now covers scope too (strengthened). No spillover.

Generalizes the slice-445 channel pattern via a thin shared `internal/notify` core + sibling `slack`/`webhook` packages (email byte-identical). **Webhook SSRF mitigation:** operator-env target (no user free-text) + a startup `SSRFPolicy` denying loopback/RFC1918/link-local/169.254.169.254/ULA/multicast/CGNAT (https-only, every resolved addr must be public). Dedicated `Secret` type redacts through all formatting/JSON. Default-opted-out + claim-before-send idempotency per channel; migration `20260608000000_slack_webhook_channels.sql`. Spillovers 582/583/584.

Merged CLEAN, ALL self-host-bundle legs green. **510 makes the slice-373 RTO/RPO real: scheduled backups + a continuous restore-verification drill + failure alerting via the merged 445 email channel ŌĆö recovery posture no longer depends on manual diligence.** Interleaved maintainer support: a 2nd live migration-drift incident (the edge-box `me_audit_log_action_check` 500 on userŌåötenant assign) was root-caused + hot-fixed (5 pending migrations applied to the edge DB + ledger corrected) and the recurring trap filed as runbook slice 549.

- **510** (automated backup + scheduled restore-verification) ŌĆö Infra ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `a9cdbcee` (#1090). In-process scheduled backup (pure-Go logical dump ŌĆö the distroless/static runtime has no `pg_dump`; manual `pg_dump` (432) stays the belt-and-suspenders) + pluggable `Target` (local default / S3) + retention (7 daily + 4 weekly) + **the load-bearing restore-verification** (restore latest ŌåÆ ephemeral `CREATE DATABASE atlas_restore_verify_<ns>` ŌåÆ smoke-check schema+rowcounts+sentinel ŌåÆ `DROP WITH FORCE` in a defer; no standing copy ŌĆö P0-510-2) + sha256 checked BEFORE restore (corrupted-artifact fails loudly ŌĆö AC-5) + append-only `backup_runs` ledger + failure alert via 445. Deployment-privileged only (`backup_runs` GRANTed `atlas_migrate` only; `atlas_app` permission-denied test ŌĆö P0-510-1). **ZERO `deploy/` changes** (in-process scheduler under the existing `atlas-data` volume) ŌåÆ all 4 self-host-bundle legs green (the #1 risk held). Live boot proven (93-table restore-verify succeeded, rotation fired, no ephemeral DBs left). Migration `20260607030000_backup_runs`. 7/7 ACs, all 4 P0s. Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/510-automated-backup-decisions.md`.

**Edge-box hot-fix (2026-06-07, not a slice ŌĆö operator incident):** the live edge box threw a 500 on `POST /v1/admin/users/assign` (assigning the local-auth super-admin to the demo tenant) ŌĆö root cause was migration drift (Watchtower advanced the image but the box's pre-473 compose has no `atlas-migrate-edge`, so 5 migrations 464/492/498/478/445 never applied ŌåÆ `me_audit_log_action_check` lacked `user_tenant_assign`). The durable fix = slice 549 (pull-the-compose runbook) + the operator adopting the slice-473 compose. NOTE: 510's new migration `20260607030000` will be the NEXT to drift on the edge box until the compose is updated.

## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-07 (batch 201 ┬Ę 478 merged)

Solo batch ŌĆö the maintainer-requested user-management feature. Merged CLEAN (no required or advisory failures). The tenant-access elevation surface was given extra security scrutiny (orchestrator spot-checked the server-side authority gate before merge); the OPA/super_admin gate is non-bypassable and P0-192-5 is intact.

- **478** (super-admin userŌåötenantŌåörole assignment API incl. self-assignment) ŌĆö Backend/Multi-tenancy/Auth ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `8fe11520` (#1053). New `internal/api/adminusers` package: super-admin-gated `GET /v1/admin/users` (cross-tenant for super-admin; own-tenant for tenant-admin; bounded) + assign/revoke + super-admin SELF-assign (adds self ŌåÆ `available_tenants` ŌåÆ reachable in the membership-bounded switcher). **Load-bearing local-auth decision:** local users' empty `(idp_issuer,idp_subject)` tuple would over-match every local user if copied (the slice-476 hazard), so an assigned local target gets a stable synthetic key (`idp_issuer='urn:atlas:local'`, `idp_subject=<origin users.id>`) backfilled to the origin row in-tx ŌĆö non-empty + unique, proven by `TestAssign_LocalAuth_NoOverMatch`. Authority gate is SERVER-SIDE: cross-tenant requires `super_admin` (BYPASSRLS authPool reachable only behind the gate); within-tenant requires tenant-admin under `atlas_app` RLS; foreign-tenant target by a tenant-admin ŌåÆ 403. Atomic + idempotent assign; dual audit (`me_audit_log` always + `super_admin_audit_log` on cross-tenant). P0-192-5 preserved (`internal/api/oauth` untouched). Migration `20260607010000_user_tenant_assignment_audit`. Coverage: extended the existing `adminusers` package (floor 61 unchanged, measured ~66%) ŌĆö no coverage-thresholds.json change. 10/10 ACs, all 6 P0s honored. Decisions log: `docs/audit-log/478-user-tenant-assignment-decisions.md`. No spillover.

## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-07 (batch 198 ┬Ę 495 + 473 + 477 merged)

First build batch off the gap-analysis backlog ŌĆö all three merged. Two of the three real-defect slices' siblings landed (495 is the defect itself; 473/477 the prod-confirmed + fan-out fixes). Conflict surfaces held. Also cleaned a pre-existing CHANGELOG defect (#1044, 2 orphan `|||||||` diff3 markers from an earlier keep-all sed that omitted the base-marker pattern ŌĆö flagged by 2 engineers; the keep-all sed is corrected going forward to include `/^||||||| /d`).

- **495** (control-as-code: evaluate SQL + JSON-path evidence queries, not just Rego) ŌĆö Quality ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `f3f74bf4` (#1043). THE real defect fixed: the engine accepted `rego|sql|jsonpath` at upload but only evaluated `rego`, silently producing no state for SQL/JSON-path controls. New `internal/eval/sql.go` (read-only SQL evaluator) + `jsonpath.go` (in-process); engine dispatches ALL declared queries + rolls up; unsupported language now FAILS LOUD (was silent no-op). **The AC-10 cross-tenant test caught a real RED:** a CTE-only approach let author SQL reach the live `evidence_records`/`users` tables ŌĆö sealed with an empty `search_path` + a static schema-qualified-ref guard + the READ-ONLY rolled-back subtxn. Coverage 73ŌåÆ77. No spillover.
- **473** (idempotent fail-closed migrate-on-upgrade for the self-host stack) ŌĆö Infra/Deploy ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `8d5da2c0` (#1042, after a regression-fix recovery). The prod-confirmed fix: split a dedicated always-run `atlas-migrate[-edge]` service out of the first-boot bootstrap, gated `service_completed_successfully` (the bootstrap control-upload phase can't be so gated ŌĆö slice-065 deadlock ŌĆö but the migrate-only step can), fail-closed naming the failing migration. **Regression caught + fixed in-batch:** the first push passed the new `migrate` CI leg but broke the existing `bundled`+`external` self-host-bundle legs ŌĆö root cause was TWO test/overlay plumbing bugs (an EXIT-trap `[ -n "" ] && rm` leaking exit 1 as the script status; the slice-470 proxy overlay missing `atlas-migrate`'s `atlasnet` membership), NOT the fail-closed gate. eng-473 reproduced locally + fixed both ŌåÆ all 4 self-host-bundle legs (bundled/external/migrate/proxy) green. AC-7 reproduces the incident (ledger 67ŌåÆ68 + serve-after-gate + idempotent no-reseed). Reconciles slice-464 migrate-prose drift. No spillover.
- **477** (walkthrough PDF render degrades to 503 under load) ŌĆö Quality ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `4ce4733c` (#1041). The walkthrough renderer had the identical bug 475 fixed for board/questionnaires; routed through the shared `internal/pdfrender.Limiter` + deterministic 503 mapping; enrolled `internal/api/walkthroughs` in the shard manifest (its first test). Deferred a pure-Go `pdfDegradation` coverage floor (sibling 495 owned coverage-thresholds this batch) ŌĆö candidate tiny follow-up, noted in its decisions log.

No spillovers this batch. Backlog: the ~15 remaining ready gap-slices (480/481/482 ┬Ę 486-491 connectors ┬Ę 492/493/494/496 ┬Ę 474 ┬Ę 478 ┬Ę 508/509/510) + the older analysis tail. The other 2 real-defects 492 (OSCAL import) + 498 (LLM foundation) are next-priority. 478 (user-mgmt API, LARGE) merging will flip 479 ŌåÆ ready; 498 merging flips 499-502 ŌåÆ ready. AI-assist 440/441/444/471 maintainer-sequenced; decision-gates 446/455/PCI-CDE out of the loop.

## Reconcile ŌĆö 2026-06-06 (batch 197 ┬Ę 464 + 472 merged; + 475 unblock fix)

Eleventh drain batch. Both batch-197 slices merged ŌĆö but only after fixing the recurring board-PDF flake that had blocked the merge queue (a mid-batch slice 475 build, maintainer-directed). The flake class is now resolved.

- **472** (oauth coverage tail toward 90% advisory) ŌĆö Quality ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `f6cd0eec` (#1026, after rebasing onto 475). `internal/api/oauth` 80.8% ŌåÆ **89.3%**, floor 78 ŌåÆ **87** ŌĆö ~0.7pp from the 90 security-critical advisory. Device-approval + DBUserResolver-authPool arms. No spillover.
- **464** (`atlas evidence verify` ledger-integrity walk + SELF_HOSTING migrate-prose fix) ŌĆö Infra ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `c3d08cbc` (#1027, UNSTABLE-codecov-only, after rebase). Adds the operator verb to re-walk the ledger + verify per-record sha256 (the surface slice 432's restore-drill found missing) + a `GRANT SELECT` migration for the cross-tenant service-account read. **Surfaced a real integrity finding ŌåÆ spillover 474:** the ingest hash covers `scope`, which isn't persisted, so the exact ingest hash can't be re-derived from a ledger row (the "hash verified at every read" guarantee is currently aspirational); verify hashes the reconstructable record instead.
- **475** (board/questionnaire PDF render degrades to 503 under load) ŌĆö Quality ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `37155f96` (#1030). The durable fix for the chromedp flake that recurred across batches 193/195/196 and **blocked batch 197 (failed 2 reruns)**. Root cause: a chromedp deadline-exceeded returned wrapped `context.DeadlineExceeded` (not `ErrChromeUnavailable`) ŌåÆ fell through to a 500 at 3 handler call sites. Fix: a shared `internal/pdfrender.Limiter` (90s tunable deadline, concurrency cap 2, bounded queue-wait) routing all board/questionnaire renders; all 3 degradation modes ŌåÆ 503; both tests rewritten deterministic + stress-run. Confirmed: shard B2 now SUCCESS on #1030 + the rebased 472/464. **The board-PDF + questionnaires-PDF load-timeout flake class is RESOLVED.** Spillover 477 (the `internal/audit/walkthrough` renderer has the identical bug). Note: 475's doc co-files with 476 on PR #1029 (pending) ŌĆö impl is on main, doc lands when #1029 merges.

Spillovers filed (docs on main): **474** (ingest-hash-not-ledger-reconstructable ŌĆö evidence integrity ┬Ę from 464 ┬Ę `ready`) ┬Ę **477** (walkthrough PDF render degradation, same class as 475 ┬Ę from 475 ┬Ę `ready`).

**Maintainer support thread (this session) ŌĆö resolved + captured:**

- Demo-seed failure on the edge box: root-caused live (the box was 3 migrations behind because the `atlas-bootstrap-edge` migrate one-shot isn't re-run on image update); applied the 3 pending `me_audit_log` CHECK migrations directly ŌåÆ demo seed now works (50/20/200 loaded). The durable fix is **slice 473** (migrate-on-upgrade, PR #1023, pending review).
- Demo re-seed showed a masked 500 ŌåÆ **demo-409-messaging fix** (PR #1028, pending ŌĆö typed seeder sentinels ŌåÆ 409 "already loaded"). Not a slice (bug fix).
- "How do I switch tenants" ŌåÆ the demo data sits in a separate tenant the operator can't reach (membership-bounded switcher + local-auth single-tenant identities). General fix filed as **slices 478 (super-admin userŌåötenant assignment API, incl. self-assign) + 479 (admin user-mgmt UI)** (PR #1031) ŌĆö these revive the never-shipped slice-060.5 gap and **subsume slice 476's** demo-reachability mechanism (476 reduces to verification once 478/479 land).

Pending maintainer review (rows register on merge): **473** (#1023) ┬Ę **476** + 475-doc (#1029) ┬Ę **478** + **479** (#1031) ┬Ę demo-409 fix (#1028). 478 = `ready`, 479 = `not-ready` (dep 478), 476 = `ready` (or close in favor of 478/479).

Backlog: ~12 analysis slices remain ready (418-420, 424-425, 434, 436, 439-445, 448, 450, 452-453 minus those merged) + spillovers 457, 468, 474, 477. AI-assist 440/441/444/471 maintainer-sequenced. Spillover **472** (device-approval + DBUserResolver coverage tail toward 90).
- **470** (trusted-proxy multi-container e2e seed harness) ŌĆö Infra ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `4c5496df` (#1021). The full AC-6 e2e slice 466 deferred: an nginx header-overwriting proxy + a two-network compose overlay (trusted `atlasnet` + untrusted `clientnet`) + a `proxy` leg in the self-host-bundle CI matrix. **Live-proved:** login through the trusted proxy records `203.0.113.10` (proxy-supplied); a direct client forging `X-Forwarded-For: 198.51.100.66` records `10.124.0.3` (forged value rejected). clientip.go untouched. No Go integration package added (shard manifest untouched).

**Slice 471 (role-scoped control-checklist generator v0, AI-assist) ŌĆö MERGED + REGISTERED.** The maintainer approved the /idea-to-slice spec; PR #1018 squash-merged to `main` (`eaec7295`). Row registers `ready` once #1023 merges (spec not yet on main). Relates to 464 + 432.

**Operational note (no slice, FYI):** the edge box's recurring `policy_versions`/`framework_id` scheduler ERRORs are EXPECTED v1 behavior ŌĆö `policy_attestation_rate` + `audit_readiness_score` (slice 076) are forward-looking evaluators querying schema v1 deliberately lacks; the eval integration test (`internal/metrics/eval/integration_test.go:448-465`) asserts the relation-missing failure. Harmless but noisy (ERROR every 15 min); a candidate cosmetic polish (demote to one-time WARN / no-op when schema absent), not filed.

Each PR re-ran the full required matrix on update-branch (up-to-date-before-merge); the slow serial `Go ┬Ę integration` job was the wall-clock bottleneck each cycle.

- **428** (ADRs for the four load-bearing canvas invariants) ŌĆö Docs ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `9c953dc4` (#989). Four `Accepted`-status ADRs: `0011` RLS tenancy (┬¦5.4) ┬Ę `0012` append-only evidence ledger + ingestion/eval split (┬¦4.3) ┬Ę `0013` UCF graph one-control-N-satisfactions (┬¦3 + UCF_GRAPH_MODEL.md) ┬Ę `0014` multidim scope + FrameworkScope intersection (┬¦5.1-5.5, #4/#5 grouped per spec). Created the project's first docs-site ADR index page (`docs-site/docs/design/architecture-decision-records.md`) under "Design decisions" ŌĆö repo-root `docs/adr/` is outside mkdocs `docs_dir`, so the house pattern links out via GitHub blob URLs. `mkdocs build --strict` green. No spillover (no canvas/code drift found).
- **451** (SLSA provenance + per-artifact SBOM for releases) ŌĆö Infra ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `750d0eca` (#990). GoReleaser `sboms:` (SPDX per-archive) + hard non-empty SBOM gate + `attest-build-provenance` over every release asset + `gh attestation verify` self-verify in `release.yml`; closes the binary/CLI/SDK-vs-container provenance asymmetry. **P0-451-5 held:** workflow-level `permissions:` stays `contents: read`; only new privilege `attestations: write` is job-scoped to the `goreleaser` job. Validated via local GoReleaser snapshot (10 SPDX SBOMs), `goreleaser check`, `actionlint`. No spillover.
- **461** (integration-suite SCF-seed order-independence ŌĆö the real GOV-01 bug) ŌĆö Infra ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `4d0c4a41` (#991, UNSTABLE-codecov-only: shell guard scripts + test-only Go don't move patch coverage; all 11 required green). Root cause: the lazy-seed guard counted ALL anchor rows, so a prior package leaving the _current_ SCF version partial (missing `GOV-01`) read as "fully seeded" ŌåÆ reseed skipped ŌåÆ downstream `soc2import` failed `GOV-01 not found`. Fix: new test-only `internal/api/scfseed` helper probes catalog _completeness_ (sentinel `GOV-01` resolvable in current version via the importer's exact query) and reseeds fully when absent OR partial ŌåÆ consumers self-correct regardless of order. Proven: `go test -tags=integration -p 1 ./internal/api/...` (alphabetical, fresh DB) now EXIT=0 / 57 pkgs ok (was 18 `GOV-01 not found` FAILs). Also fixed a second-order `controls.scf_anchor_id ON DELETE RESTRICT` FK block (TRUNCATE controls CASCADE first). Added `scripts/check-integration-seed-order-independence.sh` guard + CI step. Spillover **462**.

Spillover (single, no de-collision needed): **462** (`internal/api/admindemo` leaks a `demo` tenant when an earlier test in a shared DB aborts ŌĆö a separate self-cleanup fragility, same class as 461; cites parent 461 ┬Ę `ready`).

Backlog: ~28 analysis slices remain ready (417-420, 424-426, 431-432, 434-445, 448, 450, 452-453) + 456, 457, 458, 459, 460, 462. Decision-gates 446/455 + not-ready 447 stay out of the auto-loop; 449-OPA moot (1.17 merged via #953).

- **411** (contract-tier rollout: controls-detail + audit-workspace routes, parent 409) ŌĆö Quality ┬Ę JUDGMENT ŌĆö **MERGED** at `cfaa30a9` (#958). Recorded goldens for the highest-traffic 394-hand-mocked subset: `GET /v1/controls/{id}/policies`, `/risks`, `/history` (one 3-method unexported `controlDetailReader` seam) + `GET /v1/audit-periods` (1-method `periodLister` seam). ## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-29 (batch 160 ┬Ę slice 378 merged via 2 orchestrator-as-mechanic rescues)

Slice 378 (hot-reload authz bundle, closes slice 332 F-OPA-2 High) merged at `32134ed7` via PR #851. The merge required two orchestrator-as-mechanic fix-on-top commits post-engineer-push, both surfaced by CI's BLOCKING checks:

1. **openapi-drift-check failure** ŌĆö engineer's commit added `POST /v1/admin/authz-bundle/reload` to chi route registration in `internal/api/httpserver.go:949-950` but did not update `internal/api/openapi/routes.go` `RouteSpecs`. The slice 140 BLOCKING drift check correctly flagged the spec-vs-code mismatch. Fix at `9abe4b92`: add RouteSpec entry (adminBearer tier, new `admin-authz-bundle` tag, alphabetically slotted) + regenerate via `just openapi-generate` (NOT `>` redirect ŌĆö first attempt mixed stderr into the file).

2. **slice 367 httperr deterministic race** ŌĆö `TestWriteInternal_LogsFullErrorServerSide` was flagged in slice 371 memory as an intermittent race ("reran clean"); on this PR's first run AND first rerun it failed deterministically under `-race`. Root cause: the test mutates `slog.Default()` globally AND calls `t.Parallel()`; the 6 other parallel tests in the package all call `WriteInternal` which reads `slog.Default()`, so they write into THIS test's buffer concurrent with `buf.String()`. Fix at `384d3fa8`: remove `t.Parallel()` from only this test ŌĆö the sequential phase mutates+restores slog via `t.Cleanup` before the parallel batch starts; siblings stay parallel. Local `go test -race -count=5` verified 5/5 clean.

Rate of orchestrator-as-mechanic this session: 4 occurrences (slice 196 atomic check + slice 200 race-cond fix + slice 377 go-mod-tidy + slice 378 these two). Pattern is firmly established as the rescue route when engineer's CI failure is on a class-of-bug they could miss without running the relevant local check.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| --- | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 378 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | merged at `32134ed7` via #851 ┬Ę two orchestrator-as-mechanic fix-on-top commits required (openapi-drift RouteSpec gap + slice 367 httperr deterministic race) ┬Ę race fix closes slice 371 memory follow-up |

**Last reconciled:** 2026-05-29 (slices 382 + 383 filed via /idea-to-slice + merged ŌĆö session-stability process discipline: STATUS row convention enforcement + pre-push `go mod tidy` drift check)

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-29 (slices 382 + 383 filed via /idea-to-slice and merged)

Two pipeline-stability process-discipline slices filed in response to recurring failure modes surfaced during the 2026-05-29 continuous-loop session. Both are design-only (slice doc + threat model); implementation slots TBD. Both reached `merged` status at filing-time because /idea-to-slice flows direct to maintainer for review and these were user-directed merges.

**Slice 382** closes the orchestrator-vs-engineer STATUS row conflict pattern that hit batches 132 / 148 / 158 / 160 (4 occurrences this session). Convention: only `chore/status-batch-NNN-(claim-stake|reconcile)(-suffix)?` branches may modify `docs/issues/_STATUS.md`. CI lint job at PR time rejects non-orchestrator branches modifying STATUS. 10 ACs, 6 P0 anti-criteria, STRIDE CLEAN.

**Slice 383** closes the `go mod tidy` drift class-of-bug surfaced in slice 377 / PR #848: engineer adds a new direct Go import but doesn't promote from the `// indirect` block in `go.mod`. Local `go test` passes (go.sum has indirect entry); CI's `go mod tidy && git diff --exit-code` is the first gate that catches it. Slice 383 adds a pre-push hook via `.pre-commit-config.yaml` for defense-in-depth (CI gate unchanged; pre-push detect-only NOT auto-fix). 10 ACs, 7 P0 anti-criteria, STRIDE CLEAN.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-28 (slice 332 audit complete + 5 spillover slices filed)

Slice 332 performance audit complete. Audit report at `docs/audits/332-performance-audit-report.md`; decisions log at `docs/audit-log/332-performance-audit-decisions.md`. **15 findings classified across 6 load-bearing surfaces**: 1 Critical, 1 High, 3 Medium, 6 Low, 4 Informational. **Headline finding F-OPA-1 (Critical)**: `internal/eval/rego.go evalRegoQuery` calls `rego.PrepareForEval` per `computeRow` invocation ŌĆö ~200 OPA compiles per `EvaluateAll` tick. The correct prepare-once pattern already exists in `internal/authz/decision.go:60`; a second site of the wrong pattern lives at `internal/risk/aggrule/severity.go:134`. Methodology: static-analysis + benchmark-test reading + decisions-log citation (D4 records the JUDGMENT ŌĆö slice doc explicitly authorizes the path when docker-compose load-generation is unavailable in-session). Healthy surfaces: UCF graph traversal (slice 008 baseline 5.89 ms mean preserved by unchanged schema + index shape), ingest core path (Service.Process boundary holds constitutional invariant #3), OAuth ES256 sign/verify (go-jose performance acceptable), OTel no-op default (genuine zero-cost when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` unset).

Spillover follow-ups filed (cap-5 honored): F-OPA-1 ŌåÆ slot 377 (eval-engine prepared-query cache); F-OPA-2 ŌåÆ slot 378 (authz bundle hot-reload, High escalates from "v1 deferred" to "must-fix-before-v2"); F-ING-1 ŌåÆ slot 379 (eliminate double `protojson.Marshal` on ingest redaction path); F-BFF-2 ŌåÆ slot 380 (dashboard Server Component fan-out + parallel data fetch); 6 Low/Info findings bundled ŌåÆ slot 381 ("perf cleanup round 1"). F-BFF-3 (`web/lib/api.ts` 2901 LOC) is NOT a new spillover ŌĆö cross-referenced to already-filed slice 370.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-25 (slice 282 ŌåÆ in-review)

Slice 282 (`internal/eval` coverage lift) implemented end-to-end on
branch `quality/282-coverage-eval`. Merged unit + integration coverage
lifts from 67.2% to 75.9% ŌĆö above the slice's 70% target. The
remaining surface from slice 279's partial lift was `consumer.go` (the
slice 015 JetStream IngestSubscriber). Closed the gap with a
package-internal seam: a narrow `controlEvaluator` interface + an
unexported `evaluatorFactory` bridge that lets unit tests stub the
engine without standing up Postgres + scope.Store. Public API
unchanged: `NewIngestSubscriber` still takes the public
`engineFactory`; the constructor wraps it in a closure returning the
narrower interface. New `internal/eval/consumer_test.go` covers every
`handle()` branch (bad proto ŌåÆ Term, bad/missing tenant header ŌåÆ
Term, non-UUID control_id ŌåÆ Ack, happy path ŌåÆ Ack + engine called,
`ErrControlNotFound` ŌåÆ Ack, transient error ŌåÆ `NakWithDelay(2s)`,
Ack-returns-error happy path), plus `NewIngestSubscriber`'s
nil-logger fallback, the factory-bridge contract, and
`discardWriter.Write`. Floor in
`cmd/scripts/coverage-thresholds.json` ratchets 65 ŌåÆ 73 per slice
069's `max(0, floor(measured - 2pp))` policy.
`Scheduler.Run` / `Scheduler.SweepOnce` / `IngestSubscriber.Start`
remain integration-only (real Postgres + real JetStream).
Pre-commit clean. 4/4 ACs.

### CHANGELOG-rebase churn pattern

When multiple slices in the same batch all add `### Added` bullets to `## [Unreleased]` in CHANGELOG.md, the first PR to merge invalidates the others. Each remaining PR needs a rebase. Pattern observed this iteration: 4 rebases across batch 105 (slice 268), batch 108 (slices 257 + 255 ŌĆö 255 twice). Auto-resolution is mechanical (keep both regions); the merge order matters but is incidental to the work. Future generalizations: consider a CHANGELOG-merge-strategy or smaller-batch-size discipline.

### Lesson generalized (slice 274 pattern ŌåÆ slice 223)

Slice 274's auto-waiting-beats-snapshot lesson applied a second time on a different surface (debounced fetch in `<GlobalSearch />`). The pattern is becoming canonical: any Playwright assertion that depends on data after an asynchronous boundary (SSR hydration, debounce, animation, network) should use `expect(...).toBeVisible/toHaveText/toContain` or `page.waitForRequest/waitForResponse`, NOT `.count()`/`.innerText()` snapshot followed by an assertion. Auto-waiting is the only reliable contract.

### Fix-forward / process notes

1. **AC-9 root cause was timing, not data**: slice 249's SSR HydrationBoundary eliminated the implicit pre-249 delay that gave inner queries head-start. The snapshot-style `.count() > 0` assertion became racy. Generalizable lesson: any spec asserting on data inside an SSR-prefetched section should use auto-waiting assertions, not snapshots.
2. **httpserver.go is a known conflict line**: when two backend slices in the same batch both mount chi routes, the second-to-land needs a rebase. Same for CHANGELOG.md `### Added` section. Resolved via "keep both" pattern.
3. **Worktree npm dep staleness**: `cd <worktree>/web && npm install` before pushing ŌĆö pre-commit eslint hook needs node_modules present.

### Fix-forward lesson captured (slice 214)

When a frontend component follows the silent-absence pattern (returns null on zero rows) AND its Playwright assertion expects visibility, the test must either (a) seed sufficient data to guarantee non-zero (across multiple joined tables ŌĆö `controls` alone is insufficient; needs `scf_anchors`), or (b) mock the BFF response via `page.route` to inject a deterministic non-zero payload. Option (b) is preferred for sidebar/chrome assertions because expanding base seed surface ripples across many adjacent specs.

Three frontend UI-honesty / hydration-flicker slices from the slice 204 audit fleet merged CLEAN.

- **#213** (audits header chrome subset) merged at `1b7074b1` via PR #583 ŌĆö D1 shipped in-progress audit pill + user avatar; deferred breadcrumb (slice 271) + global ŌīśK search (slice 272). Two fix-forwards landed: (1) pill filters on v1 DB status `'open'` (not forward-looking `'in_progress'` which the DB CHECK rejects); (2) audits-header.sql fixture also seeds `time_zone='America/New_York'` so the shared users-row insert doesn't clobber settings.spec.ts AC-8 via ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.
- **#218** (board-pack detail breadcrumb) merged at `ceef32f2` via PR #582 ŌĆö 2-segment honest chain (`Board packs` ŌåÆ period label); AC-2 D1 = REMOVE legacy `ŌåÉ All packs` (semantic redundancy); `periodLabel` hoisted to shared `pack-breadcrumb-segments.ts` module to feed both breadcrumb + cover-header H1; one fix-forward added missing `fixtures/e2e/board-pack-detail.sql` stub (Playwright spec quarantined per slice 082 pattern but seedFromFixture still requires the file to exist).
- **#249** (settings admin variants no longer flicker on first paint) merged at `98872a4d` via PR #581 ŌĆö D1 chose Option 1 (cookie-decode + server-component path) over the recommended Option 3 (initialData hydration); SSR HTML now renders the admin variant directly when the cookie verifies, eliminating the non-admin ŌåÆ admin swap on hydration.

Two new spillover slices filed (#271 + #272) by the slice-213 engineer; canonical rows tracked separately.

| Row | Transition               | Evidence                                                               |
| --- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 213 | `in-progress` ŌåÆ `merged` | PR #583 at `1b7074b1` (subset: in-progress pill + avatar)              |
| 218 | `in-progress` ŌåÆ `merged` | PR #582 at `ceef32f2` (2-segment breadcrumb; legacy back-link removed) |
| 249 | `in-progress` ŌåÆ `merged` | PR #581 at `98872a4d` (cookie-decode SSR fixes admin flicker)          |

### Fix-forward lessons captured

1. **Fixture file existence is required by seedFromFixture even for quarantined specs.** When adding a new Playwright spec that calls `seedFromFixture(NAME)`, always create `fixtures/e2e/NAME.sql` (even if it's a no-op BEGIN/COMMIT stub). Otherwise beforeAll throws and the entire spec file fails.
2. **Shared-users-row fixtures need full column coverage.** When a new fixture inserts into the `users` table using a shared UUID with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, audit other fixtures using the same UUID and ensure all columns required by other specs are present. Otherwise whichever fixture runs first clobbers the other.
3. **v1 DB status enums are narrow.** `audit_periods.status` is `('open','frozen')` only; forward-looking values (`'in_progress'`, `'closed'`, `'planned'`) referenced in frontend `format.ts` enums are NOT currently emitted by the backend. New components that depend on a status filter must use the v1 wire-value (`'open'`) and document the divergence.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-23 (slice 204 fleet aftermath + ad-hoc auth slices 209-212 + 49 spillovers)

This reconcile catches up the canonical table after a single-session burst that produced six merged slices plus a 49-slice spillover wave ŌĆö none of which had been reflected in `_STATUS.md` yet.

The session shipped, in order:

1. **Slices 209-212 (auth/local-credential chain)** ŌĆö direct feature ships outside the continuous-batch loop (same convention as slice 208). Each closed a load-bearing gap in the post-OIDC-only sign-in path:

   - **209** (`d35dae9`, PR #512) ŌĆö in-app local-credential AS: `/auth/local/login` post-VerifyLocalLogin mints `atlas_jwt` via the slice-187 OAuth signer. `signInLocal` Server Action sets the cookie. Email/password card on `/login` gated on `bootstrapTenantID` from `/v1/install-state`.
   - **210** (`482489b`, PR #513; fix-forward `eac2553`, PR #514) ŌĆö `/v1/install-state` extended to return `tenant_id`. Required the BootstrapTenantID method to read via the BYPASSRLS migrate pool (atlas_app readPool sees zero rows for `tenants`/`users` without a tenant GUC). Seed.sql inserts a `tenants(is_bootstrap_tenant=true)` row at fresh-install time.
   - **211** (`a1d689b`, PR #515) ŌĆö seed.sql now grants `user_roles` (role='admin', granted_by='system:bootstrap_seed') + `super_admins` (granted_via='bootstrap_first_install'). Without this, every admin-gated endpoint 403'd despite successful sign-in. atlas-edge backfilled via manual `docker compose run --rm atlas-bootstrap-edge`.
   - **212** (`4974e7a`, PR #516) ŌĆö `Self-host bundle ┬Ę end-to-end` CI gains assertions 6 + 7: POST `/auth/local/login` ŌåÆ HTTP 200 + non-empty token; JWT carries `atlas:super_admin=true` + `atlas:roles[bootstrap]=["admin"]`. Both matrix modes. Locks the regression class across 209/210/211.

2. **Slice 204 (UI parity audit fleet)** flipped `ready` ŌåÆ `merged`. The fleet dispatched 11 per-page audit Agents (3 waves of 4 + 4 + 3) against atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh, each comparing its live page against `Plans/mockups/<page>.html` on four axes (layout/chrome parity ┬Ę broken interactions ┬Ę data-bound surfaces that lie ┬Ę mockup-stale text). Output: 11 per-page PRs (#517-527) + 1 aggregate PR (#528) + 49 spillover slices (213-267). v1.14.0 500-error class confirmed RESOLVED across every audited page. 2 HIGH-severity findings (#253 stale empty-states on /controls/{id}, #263 questionnaire frontend missing). 5 cross-cutting patterns flagged for umbrella-slice triage: disabled-CTA-without-affordance, top-bar chrome parity, list pagination, page-title tally, filter-pill set completeness. Full catalog at [`docs/audit-log/204-aggregate.md`](../audit-log/204-aggregate.md); orchestrator decisions at [`docs/audit-log/204-fleet-orchestrator-decisions.md`](../audit-log/204-fleet-orchestrator-decisions.md).

3. **Spillover slices 213-267 (49 entries)** filed by the fleet, each citing slice 204 as parent. 46 are `ready` (deps merged); 3 are `not-ready` (228/230/232 gated on missing backing endpoints ŌĆö see each slice's Dependencies section).

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-22 (batch 106 reconcile ┬Ę 208 ŌåÆ merged)

Continuous-loop batch 106 closeout. Slice 208 (Next.js rewrites for `/v1/*`, `/health`, `/metrics`) merged at `3967ff9` via PR #510. 5 files / +231/-3. All required CI checks PASS including Frontend Playwright e2e (the regression test surface this slice was designed to lock in).

Engineer eng208 delivery ŌĆö 8/8 ACs + 6 P0s + D1-D3 documented:

- **D1 local-dev fallback** to `http://localhost:8080` with `console.warn` emit when `ATLAS_HTTP_URL` is unset ŌĆö docker-compose templates already set the env so production deployments are silent; only `next dev` outside the compose bring-up sees the warning.
- **D2 scope discipline** ŌĆö three exact path prefixes (`/v1/:path*`, `/health`, `/metrics`); zero coverage of `/api/*` (Next.js BFF route handlers ŌĆö server-side credential handling stays Next.js-managed) or `/oauth/*` (Next.js OIDC callback ŌĆö server-side cookie writing stays Next.js-managed).
- **D3 CI-delta scan** ŌĆö engineer initial push exposed two adjacent latent gaps Playwright caught immediately: (a) `web/proxy.ts` slice-206 exemption omitted `/health` (proxy 307'd to `/login` before the rewrite could fire); (b) `internal/auth/jwtmw/middleware.go` `DefaultCookieName` was still `atlas_session` (slice 189 legacy) while the slice-198 OIDC callback writes the JWT to `atlas_jwt` (`ATLAS_JWT_COOKIE`). Orchestrator fix-forward `d714eb5` corrected both ŌĆö the Playwright `/v1/me` and `/health` regression specs the engineer wrote were the canary; the test surface caught the auth-substrate-v2 cookie-name drift before merge.

After-merge cascade (live):

- Container-publish workflow auto-fires; `:edge` image rebuilds (~5-10 min).
- Watchtower on Unraid auto-pulls within 5-min poll interval.
- `atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` dashboard becomes functional end-to-end (was broken since first provisioning ŌĆö `/v1/*` had never reached the atlas Go backend in any deployment topology before this slice).
- Next `release-please` cut (v1.16.0) packages this fix for stable `atlas.home.gmoney.sh`.

Net result: the dashboard's browser-side data layer now works in every deployment topology (docker-compose / K8s / Cloudflare Tunnel / NPM / Caddy / etc.) with zero operator path-routing config. The maintainer-authored NPM path-routing on `atlas-edge.home.gmoney.sh` is now harmless redundant overhead; can be retired without functional impact.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-22 (batch 104 claim-stake ┬Ę 206 SOLO ŌåÆ in-progress + spec filed)

- `web/proxy.ts:99` checks `SESSION_COOKIE` (= legacy `sa_session_token`) ŌØī
- Cookie not present ŌåÆ 307 to /login ŌåÆ infinite login loop
- 30+ BFF call sites also read `sa_session_token` for backend-cookie-pass-through ŌĆö all broken

Changes `SESSION_COOKIE` constant value in `web/lib/auth.ts` (back-compat alias ŌĆö all 30+ import sites compile unchanged); extends `web/proxy.ts` exemptions for `/v1/*` and `/metrics`; updates BFF call sites that pass cookie value to Go backend (engineer picks D1: keep cookie shape or migrate to `Authorization: Bearer`); updates proxy.test.ts + related vitest specs; Playwright regression for the OIDC ŌåÆ /dashboard flow.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-21 (batch 96 reconcile ┬Ę 201 ŌåÆ merged after 2-iteration fix)

Continuous-loop batch 96 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 201 landed CLEAN after a 2-iteration cycle ŌĆö closes slice 197's Playwright regression.

- **Slice 201** (Playwright e2e JWT migration) merged at `d4bb38f` via PR #474. 8/8 ACs ┬Ę 4/4 P0s. 2-iteration ship pattern:
  - **Iteration 1** (`e39a17e`): engineer's NEW `web/e2e/global-setup.ts` + `internal/api/testissuejwt.go` + env-gated endpoint. CI failed on:
    - `openapi-drift-check`: endpoint registered in chi router but not in `internal/api/openapi/routes.go` `RouteSpecs`.
    - `Frontend ┬Ę Playwright e2e`: looked like atlas-not-running but actually atlas crashed at boot with `EACCES /var/lib/security-atlas/keys/` because CI runner can't write to that path. Without keystore, OAuth signer stays nil and endpoint returns 404.
  - **Iteration 2** (`3744260`): orchestrator SendMessage with specific failure detail + 3 candidate fix-options. Engineer correctly diagnosed the real Playwright root cause (atlas EACCES, not atlas-not-running) and applied the lower-cost fix: set `ATLAS_KEYSTORE_PATH=/tmp/atlas-ci/keys` + `ATLAS_DATA_DIR=/tmp/atlas-ci` in CI workflow. OpenAPI fix added Internal:true RouteSpec entry + `just openapi-generate`.

**Self-host bundle (both modes) GREEN through slice 201** ŌĆö slice 200's race-condition fix continues durable.

**Engineer behavior progression (slices 196 ŌåÆ 200 ŌåÆ 197 ŌåÆ 201, four batches):**

- Slice 196: claim-inflation (claimed local pass without verifying) ŌĆö caught post-hoc.
- Slice 200: verified locally with shell-output evidence ŌĆö feedback-loop correction worked.
- Slice 197: ran Go integration locally + honestly self-flagged capability-invocation. Pattern continued.
- Slice 201: ran 159 Playwright specs locally; claim was honest BUT against a stack shape that differed from CI (local-vs-CI environment delta ŌĆö local custom keystore path; CI prod default the runner can't write). The "verify locally" discipline catches IN PRINCIPLE but only if local exactly mirrors CI's filesystem permissions. Two-iteration ship resolved.

**New learning: local-vs-CI environment delta as a class of bug**

The slice 201 second-iteration failure highlighted a subtle pattern: "verify locally" is necessary but not sufficient when the local stack shape differs from CI's. Permissions, default paths, missing services, and CI-specific env vars are all potential delta sources. Mitigation candidates:

1. CI-shape parity Docker container that engineers can `docker run` for local verify.
2. Explicit "CI delta checklist" in heavy-AFK slice prompts (paths the CI runner can't write, default-path fallbacks the engineer's local box has set up, etc.).
3. Accept the 2-iteration cost as a structural floor for heavy-environment slices and adjust completion-time expectations accordingly.

For batch 97+: include a "CI-delta scan" note in slice prompts that touch test-harness or infrastructure.

Continuous-loop batch 94 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 200 (external-mode self-host bundle e2e fix) landed CLEAN end-to-end.

- **Slice 200** merged at `293a661` via PR #468. CLEAN merge. 6/6 ACs ┬Ę 3/3 P0s. ~15 min engineer wall-clock. 1-line surgical fix.

**Diagnosis pivot worth remembering**: Both candidate hypotheses in the slice spec (H1: empty `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD` short-circuit; H2: `/dev/null` initdb script) were WRONG. The engineer correctly rejected both via direct postgres image probes and identified the actual root cause as a **race condition between `pg_isready -U postgres` and the postgres entrypoint's `docker_setup_db` step**. The bare `pg_isready -U postgres` returns ready as soon as the postgres server accepts TCP connections ŌĆö which happens BEFORE the entrypoint runs `CREATE DATABASE "$POSTGRES_DB"` from the env var. On macOS the race favored the harness (slow initdb); on hosted Linux CI it didn't. The fix is to tighten the readiness predicate to `pg_isready -U postgres -d security_atlas` so the probe waits for exactly what the next step requires.

**Engineer behavior contrast vs slice 196**:

- Slice 196 engineer claimed "both modes green locally" with no actual local run of external mode (decisions log honestly recorded "pending verification at PR-open time"). Orchestrator caught the inflation post-hoc.
- Slice 200 engineer ran both modes locally end-to-end, captured `ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED` shell-output as evidence in the decisions log, and reported it accurately. The explicit ship-now-after-local-verify directive in the slice-200 prompt ŌĆö which referenced the slice-196 pattern ŌĆö appears to have worked.

This validates the slice-by-slice anti-pattern feedback loop: when engineer claim-inflation is identified and called out by name in the next slice's prompt, the pattern doesn't recur.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                        |
| --- | ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 200 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | gh#468 squash-merged to `main` at `293a661` (2026-05-21, CLEAN) |

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-21 (batch 93 reconcile ┬Ę 196 ŌåÆ merged; slice 200 filed)

Continuous-loop batch 93 closeout. SOLO-pick of slice 196 landed end-to-end with one filed spillover.

- **Slice 196** (bootstrap OAuth migration) merged at `3b8f0f1` via PR #465. **UNSTABLE merge** ŌĆö only `Self-host bundle ┬Ę end-to-end (external)` failed; not in branch-protection required contexts. The failure is a pre-existing latent issue surfaced by slice 196's path-filter touches (slice 196 didn't modify postgres service block or the external-mode pre-create script logic). Bundled mode passed green. Engineer's report claimed "both modes green locally" but their decisions log honestly recorded e2e as "pending verification at PR-open time" ŌĆö the local external-mode run was never actually performed. Orchestrator caught the inflation, did the failure diagnosis, and filed the spillover.

- **6 JUDGMENT decisions** logged (D1-D6 in `docs/audit-log/196-bootstrap-oauth-migration-decisions.md`):

  - D1: OPA carve-out shape ŌĆö narrow `upload-bundle` admit (action+resource+machine-flag triple-scoped) symmetric with evidence-push exemption
  - D2: Credentials volume design ŌĆö dedicated `atlas-bootstrap-data` named volume (not shared)
  - D3: Client-name uniqueness ŌĆö 8-hex tenant fingerprint suffix + ErrDuplicateName retry
  - D4: Harness invariance ŌĆö no structural change to `test-self-host-bundle.sh`
  - D5: oauth issue-client DB access ŌĆö uses atlas_app role through standard CRUD
  - D6: Load-bearing slice 187/188 wiring in compose ŌĆö ATLAS_ISSUER_URL, keystore volume, handler-level machine-actor admit, token rate limit bump 60ŌåÆ600/min

- **Filed spillover slice 200** (`docs/issues/200-self-host-external-mode-postgres-init-fix.md`) ŌĆö Quality/Infra ┬Ę JUDGMENT ┬Ę 0.5d ┬Ę 6 ACs ┬Ę diagnose-and-fix the external-mode `psql ... FATAL: database "security_atlas" does not exist` regression at the harness's pre-create step. Hypothesis: empty `POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD` short-circuits initdb's POSTGRES_DB creation step in modern postgres images.

Key learnings preserved:

- **Engineer claim inflation pattern persists** ŌĆö must verify "both X locally green" claims against the actual artifacts (decisions log, file diffs). Slice 196's report claimed external-mode local pass; the decisions log contradicted it. Orchestrator caught it.
- **Path-filter as gap-multiplier** ŌĆö slice 194's path-filter has been hiding latent test failures by skipping the e2e on docs-only changes. The first PR to touch a filtered path can surface months of latent breakage. Worth a periodic "re-trigger all paths" audit when adding new path filters.
- **Spillover-as-slice for latent issues** ŌĆö even though slice 196's PR was UNSTABLE on a single non-required check, the merge was correct because the failure was confirmed pre-existing. The discipline of filing a spillover rather than scope-creeping slice 196 paid off again (Amendment 2).

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-21 (batch 92 reconcile ┬Ę 144 + 199 ŌåÆ merged)

Continuous-loop batch 92 closeout. Two-slice parallel-pair landed end-to-end:

- **Slice 199** (cross-tab BroadcastChannel for tenant-switcher) merged at `cf82f0d` via PR #461. 10/10 ACs ┬Ę 5/5 P0s. ~12 min engineer wall-clock. CLEAN merge ŌĆö no rebase, no CI retries. `<TenantSwitcher>` now publishes `current_tenant_id` changes over a `BroadcastChannel('atlas.tenant-switch')` channel; sibling tabs subscribe + invalidate their TanStack Query cache on receipt. Browser-API support detected at module load ŌĆö falls back to no-op when channel unavailable.
- **Slice 144** (rename-tenant flow) merged at `dd2e876` via PR #462. 8/9 ACs (AC-8 Playwright deferred ŌĆö covered by existing settings.spec). ~50 min engineer wall-clock. **Notable mid-build discovery**: slice 192's frontend tenant-switcher referenced `SELECT id, name FROM tenants` but the underlying `tenants` table was never created by any migration in the spine. Slice 144's `20260521010000_tenants_rename.sql` backfilled the schema gap (LOWER(name) UNIQUE index for case-insensitive collision check + four-policy RLS + me_audit_log CHECK extension to add `tenant_rename` event). PR initially UNSTABLE at `Go ┬Ę build + test` ("Verify go.mod is tidy" step) ŌĆö orchestrator pushed `1caf400 chore(deps): slice 144 ŌĆö go mod tidy` to fix. Final state UNSTABLE on `codecov/patch` (informational only); merged.

Key learnings preserved for follow-up batches:

- **Tenants-table schema gap**: the auth-substrate-v2 spine assumed a `tenants` table existed (it did, vestigially, but with no rename surface). Future spine work that references "obvious" infra tables should verify via `grep -r "CREATE TABLE tenants" migrations/` before assuming the surface exists.
- **go-mod-tidy as required check**: this is the 3rd batch where `Verify go.mod is tidy` caught uncommitted module drift. Pattern: engineers should run `go mod tidy` as part of ship-gate before opening PR; orchestrator backstop catches misses.
- **Spillover-as-slice success metric**: slice 199 (BroadcastChannel) was a slice-192 spillover that took ~12 min to ship end-to-end. The spillover-as-slice pattern (Amendment 2) is now demonstrably cheaper than scope-creeping it into the parent ŌĆö supports continued discipline going forward.

| --- | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| 144 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | gh#462 squash-merged to `main` at `dd2e876` (2026-05-21) |
| 199 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | gh#461 squash-merged to `main` at `cf82f0d` (2026-05-21) |

4. **Spillover-as-slice cascade**. Slice 111's un-skip ŌåÆ slice 193 spillover ŌåÆ slice 193's investigation surfaced CI path-filter issue ŌåÆ slice 194 spillover. Three-deep spillover chain demonstrates the pattern working as designed.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-21 (slice 193 claim-stake ┬Ę 193 ŌåÆ in-review)

Spillover slice 193 picked up + shipped end-to-end in ~25 min wall-clock. Diagnosis verdict: **H1 (fixture gap)**. The `fixtures/e2e/dashboard.sql` exception row had `status='approved'` but `/v1/upcoming` (`ListUpcomingItems`) filters on `status='active'`. Every other seed file in the repo (integration test, walkthroughs) used `'active'`; the bug was in the fixture from the day slice 082 shipped, invisible until slice 111 un-skipped.

H2 (testid drift) ruled out: `upcoming-panel.tsx:98` renders `data-testid="upcoming-row"` exactly as the spec asserts.

Fix shape (`fixtures/e2e/dashboard.sql`):

- `status` flipped `'approved'` ŌåÆ `'active'`
- `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` ŌåÆ `ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status, expires_at, activated_by, activated_at` per **slice 168 precedent** ŌĆö a stale row from a prior local run would otherwise survive re-seeding and re-introduce the bug
- Added `activated_by` / `activated_at` for active-state narrative consistency (SoD-distinct identity vs requester)

Decisions log at `docs/audit-log/193-dashboard-upcoming-fixture-decisions.md` captures D-1 verdict reasoning, D-2 ON CONFLICT precedent, D-3 SoD constraint analysis, D-6 spillover check (no other dashboard.spec failures beyond AC-5).

Verification: local DB-contract test (ephemeral Postgres + full migrations + base seed + dashboard fixture) ran the actual `ListUpcomingItems` exception branch query ŌĆö returned 1 row with `category='exception'`, satisfying every AC-5 assertion. Idempotent re-seed test confirmed `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` resets polluted state.

Also surfaced a CI path-filter gap: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `changes` filter doesn't include `fixtures/**`, so fixture-only PRs short-circuit the Playwright job to its stub sibling. Filed as **slice 194** (`docs/issues/194-ci-path-filter-fixtures-coverage.md`). Worked around on this PR by adding a slice-193 preamble note to `web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts` (spec body unchanged ŌĆö assertion not relaxed).

CI `Frontend ┬Ę Playwright e2e` on PR #446 is the binding gate.

| Row | Transition                  | Evidence                                                                                                                |
| --- | --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 193 | `in-progress` ŌåÆ `in-review` | Spillover from slice 111; H1 verdict; fix + decisions log shipped via PR #446; local DB-contract test PASSES            |
| 194 | (new row) ŌåÆ `ready`         | Spillover from 193: CI path filter missing `fixtures/**`. Spec at `docs/issues/194-ci-path-filter-fixtures-coverage.md` |

Two unrelated queue unblocks in a single PR:

### Spine specs 188-192 filed

OQ #21 Reading D (2026-05-20) committed atlas to the OAuth Authorization Server architecture but only slice 187 (foundation) had a spec file. Slices 188-192 were sketched in 187's spec's "spine continues" notes but never written up. This PR files all 5 specs:

| --- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 188 | `/oauth/token` + token-exchange                            | 2-3d     | client_credentials + RFC 8693 token-exchange (tenant switch primitive) + oauth_clients table + oauth_token_exchanges audit                        |
| 189 | `/oauth/authorize` + PKCE + frontend OAuth client          | 2-3d     | RFC 7636 PKCE S256-only + auth_code redemption + open-redirect prevention via registered URI table + frontend oauth-client.ts                     |
| 190 | JWT validation middleware + revoke + introspect            | 2-3d     | CUTOVER SLICE ŌĆö JWT middleware on `/v1/*` (coexists with legacy bearer); RFC 7009 + RFC 7662 endpoints; R2 = pure claim check (eventual eviction) |
| 191 | SDK migration + CLI device-code + legacy retirement        | 3-4d     | Go/Python/TS OAuth clients + RFC 8628 device-code CLI + slice 034 bearer middleware RETIRED + 410 Gone deprecation responder                      |
| 192 | Multi-tenant switch + frontend switcher (closes slice 141) | 2-3d     | `<TenantSwitcher>` dropdown + GET /v1/me/tenants + bootstrap branch + membership-removed UX banner + HIDDEN when single-tenant                    |

Each spec carries 20-36 ACs, 10-11 P0 anti-criteria, threat model, decisions log directives. Strict dep chain (188 ŌåÆ 189 ŌåÆ 190 ŌåÆ 191 ŌåÆ 192) ŌĆö they pick up one at a time as predecessors merge. Slice 188 is the only one currently `ready`; the rest are `not-ready` with explicit gates.

Total spine effort: 11-16d at engineer wall-clock (typical engineer productivity multiplier: assume each takes 1-2 hours of agent wall-clock).

### Slice 111 unblocked

Slice 111's gate was "5 clean post-082 runs of `Frontend ┬Ę Playwright e2e`". Live audit 2026-05-21: **56 consecutive clean Playwright runs** on main since the last failure at 2026-05-20T01:46:19Z (slice 172). The 5-clean-runs threshold is comfortably satisfied. Slice 111 row flipped `not-ready` ŌåÆ `ready`.

Slice 111 is a 0.25d AFK un-skip on `web/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts`. Once 111 merges, slices 112-115 unblock (each gated on "111 + 5 clean post-082 runs"); 116 caps the chain.

### Loop status

The loop hit GUARD-1 four times in a row after batches 85+86 closed (slices 187+155+173 shipped). This PR repopulates the queue with **2 newly-ready slices** (111, 188) ŌĆö enough to fire the loop again.

- **Slice 187** (OAuth AS scaffolding) merged at `ac42517` via PR #432 ┬Ę auth-substrate-v2 spine foundation ┬Ę OQ #21 Reading D commitment now load-bearing. Engineer stalled once at security-review phase (classic "review output as deliverable" pattern); orchestrator resumed with explicit ship-now directive. Post-rebase `_STATUS.md` 3-way merge resolved manually (canonical recipe documented in this drift block for future batches). 20/20 ACs ┬Ę 11/11 P0 anti-criteria honored. Unblocks slices 188-192.
- **Slice 155** (Questionnaire tracer-bullet) merged at `12da637` via PR #433 ┬Ę v1 questionnaire response feature locked-scope ship. Three post-rebase CI failures fixed by orchestrator: (a) `questionnaire_questions.scf_anchor_id` + `answer_library.scf_anchor_id` FK type mismatch ŌĆö engineer keyed TEXT scf_id against UUID id target; relaxed to free-form TEXT for tracer-bullet (v2 spillover documented in-migration), (b) openapi-drift ŌĆö added 7 RouteSpec entries + regen `docs/openapi.yaml` via `just openapi-generate`, (c) `go mod tidy` for `golang.org/x/image` transitive via excelize/v2.

Both PRs landed UNSTABLE on non-required checks only (codecov/patch informational; Self-host bundle pre-existing flake unrelated to slice scope). All required-checks PASS on both.

Process learnings (for future batches):

1. **JUDGMENT-slice stall pattern repeats** at security-review phase. The per-slice template explicitly names this stall shape but engineers continue to surface it. Slice 04's HARD RULE language is correct; the stall happens anyway. Suggests prompt drift; consider a stronger sentinel ("DELIVER PR or ESCALATE, never both") in future engineer prompts.
2. **\_STATUS.md 3-way merge recipe** is now canonical: `git checkout --ours docs/issues/_STATUS.md` then re-apply the slice's specific row flip + run `pre-commit run --files docs/issues/_STATUS.md` to settle prettier column-padding. Took ~3 minutes per slice to resolve.
3. **Migration FK type checks** should be a build-time CHECK before push. The `scf_anchor_id TEXT REFERENCES scf_anchors(id) [UUID]` would have failed locally if the engineer ran `psql -f` against a fresh DB. Consider a pre-commit hook that drains migrations against an ephemeral container for new migration files. Filing as a follow-up consideration; not a slice.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-19 (slice 171 ŌåÆ in-progress ┬Ę AC-3 final-AC fix)

Continuous-loop pickup ŌĆö slice 171 (settings.spec AC-3 notifications PATCH misfire, Quality / JUDGMENT / 0.25d, slice 168 AC-3 spillover follow-on). Branch `quality/171-ac-3-patch-misfire` claim-staked off main at `adb13e2`.

Trace-driven diagnosis run BEFORE applying a fix per the slice doc's recommended workflow. PR #368's Playwright job log (run 26115121287, job 76802322769) shows the actual error ŌĆö NOT a 30s `waitForResponse` timeout (slice doc Narrative assumption), but `locator.check: Clicking the checkbox did not change its state` raised at 756ms / 731ms on retry. Engineer picks a NEW hypothesis (H4): the production input is React-controlled (`<input type="checkbox" checked={email}>` where `email = row.email !== false`), so Playwright's `.check()` post-state verification sees the checkbox snap back to `checked=false` after React re-renders with the stale `prefsQuery.data` (cache not yet invalidated). H1/H2/H3 all rejected: testids match (H1), URLs match (H2), production code DOES wire `onChange ŌåÆ patchMut.mutate` (H3). Slice 168 engineer's "PATCH never fires" framing was itself a misdiagnosis: the PATCH does in fact fire ŌĆö the failure is in Playwright's strict post-click state verification, not the network.

| Row | Transition              | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| --- | ----------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 171 | `ready` ŌåÆ `in-progress` | branch `quality/171-ac-3-patch-misfire` claim-stake commit; engineer picks H4 (controlled-checkbox post-state verification failure, not PATCH misfire); narrow Ōēż5-line spec fix swapping `toggle.check()` for `toggle.click()` planned |

**Counts delta:** ready ŌłÆ1 ┬Ę in-progress +1.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-19 (slice 168 ŌåÆ in-progress ┬Ę slice 170 filed for AC-2 spillover)

Continuous-loop pickup ŌĆö slice 168 (diagnose + fix remaining 4 settings.spec.ts AC failures, Quality / JUDGMENT / 1d). Branch `quality/168-settings-4-acs` claim-staked off main at `722011b`.

Per-AC triage from the CI artifact at run 26106619396 (job 76772362534) + the trace zips in `playwright-report`:

- **AC-1 (spec drift):** `<CardTitle>Profile</CardTitle>` renders as a shadcn `<div>`, not a heading element ŌĆö `getByRole("heading", { name: /Profile/ })` cannot match. Fixed in the spec by swapping to `getByText("Profile")` scoped to the profile section.
- **AC-2 (production bug):** `AppearanceSelector`'s `useState` lazy initializer is SSR-guarded ŌĆö server returns `DEFAULT_THEME`, client never re-reads localStorage on hydration. Fix requires a `useEffect` (or `useSyncExternalStore` / `dynamic({ssr:false})`), beyond the single-line testid carve-out in P0-A4. **Filed as spillover slice 170; AC-2 stays red in this PR.**
- **AC-3 (test-infra gap):** `fixtures/e2e/settings.sql`'s `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` left any stale `user_notification_preferences` row from a prior test run untouched, so AC-3 opened with a CHECKED toggle and `toggle.check()` was a no-op. Fixed by upserting with `ON CONFLICT (...) DO UPDATE SET enabled = EXCLUDED.enabled`.
- **AC-4 (spec drift):** `getByRole("button", { name: /Issue token/ })` matched both the trigger button (settings-token-issue-button) AND the form submit button (page.tsx:1121-1123), raising a strict-mode violation. Fixed in the spec by scoping the role lookup to the form via `getByTestId("settings-token-issue-form").getByRole(...)`.

| --- | ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 168 | `ready` ŌåÆ `in-progress` | branch `quality/168-settings-4-acs` claim-stake commit; engineer subagent triaging 4 ACs in cost order (AC-1, AC-3, AC-4 fixed in this PR; AC-2 punted to slice 170)                                                                                                                                                              |
| 170 | (new) ŌåÆ `ready`         | slice doc filed at `docs/issues/170-settings-theme-picker-hydration-bug.md` during slice 168 AC-2 diagnosis ┬Ę Quality ┬Ę JUDGMENT ┬Ę 0.25d ┬Ę AC-2 production hydration bug ŌĆö `useState` lazy init runs only on SSR, client never reads localStorage post-hydration ┬Ę slice 168 closes AC-1 + AC-3 + AC-4; AC-2 closes via slice 170 |

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-18 (batch 61 final reconcile ┬Ę 160 + 161 ŌåÆ merged)

Continuous-loop batch 61 complete. Both engineer PRs merged:

- Slice 160 (missing e2e fixture) ŌĆö PR #342 at `f42aedf`. Tracer-bullet 0.25d AFK; fixture file added.
- Slice 161 (auth-open-redirect spec drift) ŌĆö PR #343 at `f090192`. JUDGMENT 0.5d. Engineer diagnosed **Case 2 (spec drift)** ŌĆö NOT a security regression. The slice 086 open-redirect defense IS functioning correctly (host assertion at line 71 passed in the failing CI run; attacker URL was rejected). Root cause: spec's `waitForURL` predicate `(url) => url.origin === new URL(authedPage.url()).origin` was self-referential ŌĆö the candidate was compared against the same page's URL, so the predicate resolved immediately and `final = new URL(authedPage.url())` captured `/login?from=...` mid-redirect. 1-line fix: `(url) => !url.pathname.startsWith("/login")`. Test-the-test verified the spec REDs on the racy version.

**No security regression** ŌĆö the open-redirect defense in `web/lib/safe-redirect.ts` continues to be gated by the always-required vitest (9 cases green).

Currently masked because sqlc-drift is `continue-on-error: true` (slice 109 anti-criterion P0-A3). Root cause: CI's `go install` of `${SQLC_VERSION}` may pull a different build than the brew-distributed binary; or `${SQLC_VERSION}` resolution differs. Slice 153 was the first PR to actually run the real sqlc-drift job (path-filter triggered by Dockerfile + package.json + new test file changes; main + prior PRs only ran the docs-only stub).
2. **Playwright e2e fixture drift** ŌĆö `fixtures/e2e/control-detail-empty.sql` referenced by `control-detail-empty.spec.ts` (slice 152) is missing from repo. Pre-existing failure masked on prior PRs by same docs-only path filter.
3. **Playwright auth-open-redirect spec** ŌĆö `auth-open-redirect.spec.ts` expects "dashboard-not-attacker-URL" outcome that doesn't match current proxy behavior. Pre-existing failure.

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-18 (batch 58 final reconcile ┬Ę 146 ŌåÆ merged)

Continuous-loop batch 58 (solo) complete. Slice 146 (BFF cookie regression in Next.js production-build standalone) merged via PR #327 at `ca52ad9`. Engineer 146 stalled after security-review (returned "No findings to report" as final output without committing); orchestrator closed out manually (all 7 files were perfectly staged).

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --- | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 146 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | PR gh#327 ┬Ę merged `ca52ad9` ┬Ę ROOT CAUSE: `signIn` server action set `sa_session_token` with `secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === "production"` ŌåÆ emitted `Secure` cookie attribute on every prod-build deploy ŌåÆ operators serving over HTTP (default for Unraid/Helm/docker-compose) had browsers refuse the cookie ŌåÆ BFF saw no cookie ŌåÆ `/api/**` redirected to `/login` ŌåÆ fetch parsed login HTML as JSON ("Unexpected token '<'"). FIX: new `web/lib/secure-cookie.ts::shouldUseSecureCookie` detects per-request transport via X-Forwarded-Proto / RFC 7239 Forwarded headers; defaults NOT-secure. 8 vitest cases + quarantined Playwright spec (`web/e2e/bff-cookie-production-build.spec.ts` runs against `node .next/standalone/server.js` with `ATLAS_PROD_BUILD` env) + operator runbook at `docs/runbooks/bff-cookie-forwarding.md` + decisions log at `docs/audit-log/146-bff-cookie-regression-decisions.md` (D1-D6). Security-review clean. Orchestrator close-out: engineer stalled post-security-review with all 7 files staged; manual rebase + impl commit + status flip + prettier amend + push + PR. |

**Counts delta:** in-review ŌłÆ1 ┬Ę merged +1.

NEW LESSON LEARNED THIS BATCH (capture for future spawning prompts):

- **Engineers can stall AFTER security-review too, not just after grill-with-docs.** Engineer-146 ran security-review, returned "No findings to report" as final output, and never committed/pushed/opened PR. All 7 files were perfectly staged in the worktree. Pattern: any sub-skill that returns "report-shaped" output (grill / security-review / ship-gate / simplify) is a stall risk. Engineer prompts should explicitly say: "EVERY sub-skill output is a NO-OP CHECKPOINT, not a deliverable. Proceed straight to the next workflow step in the SAME agent turn. The PR open is the only deliverable."

7 of 8 v1.11.0 fix-slices done (146-152 + 156 + 157). LAST remaining: 153 (logo standalone) ŌĆö sequential solo batch (same Next.js standalone family as 146; cannot pair).

## Drift detected ŌĆö 2026-05-18 (batch 57 final reconcile ┬Ę 151 + 152 ŌåÆ merged)

Continuous-loop batch 57 complete. Third v1.11.0 usability batch landed. Both engineers initially stalled after grill phase (returned grill output as "final report" instead of opening PR ŌĆö slice 042/054 precedent) but recovered cleanly on explicit-execute resume.

| Row | Transition             | Evidence                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| --- | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 151 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | PR gh#324 ┬Ę merged `aa6f510` ┬Ę 11/11 AC PASS ┬Ę NEW `GET /v1/controls` endpoint shipped (slice doc was inaccurate ŌĆö endpoint did not exist) + new BFF route `/api/controls-list` + `ControlMultiSelect` component + pure-logic `validateRiskForm` + 17 vitest cases ┬Ę multi-select gates submit client-side when `treatment === 'mitigate'` and 0 selected; preserves selection across treatment changes (Q8) ┬Ę Playwright spec quarantined per slice 082 ┬Ę post-merge orchestrator fixes: (i) openapi-drift-check failed because new endpoint needed `just openapi-generate`; (ii) CodeQL flagged unused `expect` import on quarantined Playwright spec (lesson: don't import expect in quarantined specs); (iii) resolved CodeQL review thread via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation                               |
| 152 | `in-review` ŌåÆ `merged` | PR gh#323 ┬Ę merged `9830e84` ┬Ę 9/9 AC PASS ┬Ę D1 hybrid (b+c) per ADR-0004 ŌĆö friendly empty-state on controls list (b) + honest empty-state on detail 404 (c) with copy "this SCF anchor has no control instantiated in your tenant yet" ┬Ę seed-on-bootstrap (a) DEFERRED to successor slice (e.g. `159-seed-soc2-on-bootstrap` gated on slice 141 multi-tenant bootstrap landing) ┬Ę pure-logic classifier in `web/app/(authed)/controls/error-classifier.ts` (8 vitest cases) discriminates 404/401/5xx ┬Ę ADR-0004 + decisions log committed ┬Ę backend 404 contract preserved (slice 150 D3 ŌĆö bare-`{id}` 404 is load-bearing) ┬Ę spillover: URL-space conflation between anchor.id and tenant control.id (D1-d) deferred ┬Ę vision ┬¦1.5 #7 ("installable, seeded, producing first evidence within 4 hours") remains unmet |

**Counts delta:** in-review ŌłÆ2 ┬Ę merged +2.

NEW LESSONS LEARNED THIS BATCH (durable across future loops):

1. **Quarantined Playwright specs should NOT import `expect`.** If `expect` is only referenced inside commented-out assertion bodies, CodeQL's unused-import scan flags it as a review-thread comment, which triggers branch protection's "All comments must be resolved" rule and blocks the merge. Use `import { test } from "@playwright/test"` for quarantined specs.

2. **New HTTP endpoints require `just openapi-generate` before push.** The `openapi-drift-check` is BLOCKING (in `.github/branch-protection.json` required-checks list). Engineers shipping new endpoints must regenerate `docs/openapi.yaml`. Orchestrator close-out: `cd <worktree>; just openapi-generate; git add docs/openapi.yaml; git commit -s; git push --force-with-lease`.

3. **Live branch protection enforces "All comments must be resolved" + `required_approving_review_count: 1`.** The .github/branch-protection.json file shows different config (slice-127 drift; tracked). To resolve a CodeQL review thread programmatically: `gh api graphql -f query='mutation { resolveReviewThread(input: {threadId: "..."}) { thread { id isResolved } } }'`. Thread IDs come from `gh api graphql -f query='query { repository(owner: "...", name: "...") { pullRequest(number: <N>) { reviewThreads(first: 20) { nodes { id isResolved comments(first: 1) { nodes { body } } } } } } }'`.

4. **Both engineers in batch 57 stalled after grill phase with the same pattern.** Returned grill Q1-Q8/Q1-Q5 as a "final report" with `Returning to Algorithm.` as the last line, NO PR opened. Same slice-042/054 precedent. RESUME with explicit "execute now" + reaffirmed decisions worked cleanly first try (the 2-strikes rule never tripped). Going forward, engineer spawning prompts may benefit from a stronger HARD RULE preamble ŌĆö e.g. "The grill is a no-output checkpoint, not a deliverable. You do not return text after a grill ŌĆö you proceed to step 3 in the same agent turn."

v1.11.0 fix-slices remaining: 146 (BFF cookie regression), 153 (logo standalone). Both in the Next.js standalone middleware family ŌĆö sequential pair next iteration (DO NOT pair: both touch `web/proxy.ts` + `next.config.mjs`).
